<doctag><picture><loc_35><loc_34><loc_63><loc_46></picture>
<section_header_level_1><loc_35><loc_61><loc_197><loc_68>2.2.8 POWER_OK2 / POWER_OK3</section_header_level_1>
<text><loc_83><loc_70><loc_442><loc_76>These pins report by default the status of the USB power delivery contract negotiation with the source.</text>
<text><loc_83><loc_78><loc_442><loc_91>Different configurations are proposed as stated in the table below to meet specific application requirements. The configuration of the POWER_OK pins can be changed by NVM programming (see Section 5 Start-up configuration).</text>
<text><loc_83><loc_94><loc_464><loc_107>Depending on the programmed configuration, they can be used in combination with VBUS_EN_SNK pin to enable different power path scenarios.</text>
<text><loc_83><loc_110><loc_464><loc_123>POWER_OK2 pin is a high voltage open drain output that allows a PMOS transistor to be directly driven to enable a power path.</text>
<text><loc_83><loc_126><loc_248><loc_132>POWER_OK3 is a low voltage open drain output.</text>
<otsl><loc_83><loc_153><loc_464><loc_401><fcel>Configuration ID<fcel>Pin name<fcel>Value<fcel>Description<nl><fcel>/NVM parameter<ecel><ecel><ecel><nl><fcel>PWR_OK_CFG[1:0]<ecel><ecel><ecel><nl><fcel>Configuration 1: all PDOs on single VBUS power path<ecel><ecel><ecel><nl><fcel>00b<fcel>VBUS_EN_SNK (1)<fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No source attached<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>Source attached<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No functionality<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No functionality<nl><fcel>Configuration 2: all PDOs on single VBUS power path + dedicated high power charging paths<ecel><ecel><ecel><nl><fcel>10b<fcel>VBUS_EN_SNK (1)<fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No source attached<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>Source attached<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No PD explicit contract<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>PD explicit contract with PDO2<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No PD explicit contract<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>PD explicit contract with PDO3<nl><fcel>Configuration 3: all PDOs on single VBUS power path + detection of USB Type-C current capability from source<ecel><ecel><ecel><nl><fcel>11b<fcel>VBUS_EN_SNK (1)<fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No source attached<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>Source attached or source supplies default USB Type-C current at 5 V when source attached<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>Source supplies 3.0 A USB Type-C current at 5 V when source attached<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No source attached or source supplies default USB Type-C current at 5V when source attached<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>Source supplies 1.5 A USB Type-C current at 5 V when source attached<nl><ucel><ucel><ucel><fcel>Not applicable<nl><fcel>01b<ecel><ecel><ecel><nl><caption><loc_205><loc_144><loc_343><loc_150>Table 4. POWER_OK pin configuration</caption></otsl>
<unordered_list><list_item><loc_84><loc_401><loc_294><loc_407>1. The VBUS_EN_SNK pin values correspond to the default behavior</list_item>
</unordered_list>
<text><loc_83><loc_414><loc_200><loc_419>In case of configuration 2 (default):</text>
<unordered_list><list_item><loc_83><loc_422><loc_459><loc_434>· When a PDO negotiation succeeds, the POWER_OK pin related to the negotiated PDO is enabled (active low) when PS_READY message is received from the source</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_436><loc_462><loc_448>· When a new PDO is negotiated upon source request, the active POWER_OK pin is disabled (Hi-Z) when the STUSB4500 sends an RDO (request data object) message to the source with the new negotiated PDO</list_item>
</unordered_list>
<page_footer><loc_35><loc_488><loc_82><loc_493>DS12499 - Rev 8</page_footer>
<page_footer><loc_437><loc_488><loc_464><loc_493>page 7/42</page_footer>
<page_header><loc_407><loc_34><loc_464><loc_42>STUSB4500</page_header>
<page_header><loc_415><loc_43><loc_464><loc_48>Pin description</page_header>
</doctag>
